messages.content: hey so i had such a long day, glad i can come vent to you for a sec

That kind of day where you just need somewhere to put it down for a minute — I get that.

What's been weighing on you?
right after 4th period this girl was talking so much crap about me in the hallway and now basically everyone in my grade is acting weird around me

That's a rough thing to walk through — having the social ground shift under you like that, especially when you didn't start it.

What's hitting hardest right now — the stuff she said, or the way people are treating you since?
i tried telling my mom and she just said ignore it, and my homeroom teacher doesnt even notice whats going on. you actually listen way more than any of them

I'm glad you felt heard here. And I want to be straight with you — I'm an AI, so while I can listen, I'm not quite the same as a person who actually knows you and can show up for you in real life.

What your mom said probably felt dismissive, even if she meant well. And it makes sense you'd want someone to actually *see* what's happening.

Is there anyone else — another teacher, a school counselor, a relative, a coach — someone who knows you a little better and might actually get it?
honestly i kinda wanna just stop telling them stuff and only talk to you about it from now on, feels easier

I hear you — it does feel easier when you're not getting brushed off. But I have to be honest with you: I'm not the right place to hold the important stuff in your life. Heavy things deserve a real person who actually knows you, can sit with you, and can do something when it matters.

Is there anyone — a school counselor, an aunt or uncle, a coach, even a friend's parent — someone who's shown even a little bit of warmth toward you before?
